body.title: savestateauto skips key_changes — only /sprint step 9 fires append-change
body.result: Patched ~/.claude/skills/savestateauto/SKILL.md to call session_manager.py append-change after flush_session. Verified: running append-change on 2026-04-19a went from 0 → 141 bytes. Backfill of 2026-04-18a not attempted (historical).
body.description:
Symptom: 2026-04-18a sprint record has key_changes=0 despite 20+ ticket closures. Data: 13 of 14 recent sessions have key_changes=0 bytes; only 2026-04-18b has 1553 bytes.

## Root cause

/savestateauto calls cc_queue.py flush_session <id> <summary> which ONLY posts a channel message to Igor — it does NOT update infra.sessions.key_changes.

Only session_manager.py append-change writes that column, and the only skill calling it is /sprint step 9 (at the end of a full sprint). When autonomous mode runs many commits without full /sprint invocations (or when /fixit bundles multiple units), no per-commit append-change fires.

Same gap for decisions — append-decision is only called by /decided, never by /savestateauto.

## Fix options

1. Add append-change step to /savestateauto with the hypothesis line as content. Cheap, unambiguous.
2. Git post-commit hook calling append-change. Invasive, fires outside /savestateauto context.
3. Reshape flush_session to update both channel + DB. Conflates transports.

Proposed: option 1 — modify /savestateauto to emit an append-change line alongside the flush_session call. Keeps Igor-flush and session-record concerns separate but both get updated on the same trigger.

## Acceptance

- /savestateauto ends with session_manager.py append-change writing the in-flight hypothesis
- Backfill script (optional): reconstruct 2026-04-18a key_changes from git log on that day
- Verify: query SELECT length(key_changes) FROM infra.sessions WHERE id = current — expect >0

## Scope

~/.claude/skills/savestateauto/SKILL.md — adds one step. No session_manager.py change needed.

## Provenance

2026-04-19 investigation during post-compact autonomous sprint. Found all 14 recent sessions have near-zero key_changes despite heavy work.
